pi_items.metadata_value: HSV color space mathematical analysis to objectively measure saturation changes
pi_items.verified_value: Verifier uses PIL's RGB→HSV conversion to extract saturation channel (S index 1), calculates mean saturation, and measures absolute/relative increases
pi_items.source: Code analysis of verifier.py lines 36-65, tested with synthetic images

pi_items.metadata_value: Mean saturation increased by at least 15% from original
pi_items.verified_value: Threshold of 15% relative increase is enforced in verifier.py line 113

pi_items.metadata_value: Saturation increase between 15% and 80% (not excessive, avoiding over-saturation)
pi_items.verified_value: Maximum 80% relative increase threshold is enforced in verifier.py line 114

pi_items.metadata_value: Less than 5% of pixels at maximum saturation (255)

pi_items.metadata_value: Mean saturation absolute increase is at least 10 points (on 0-255 scale)
pi_items.verified_value: Threshold of absolute increase >= 10 is enforced in verifier.py line 116

pi_items.verified_value: Passing 3 out of 4 criteria = 75% score, which equals the pass threshold

privileged_info_summary: The saturation_increase task uses real Unsplash landscape images (with ImageMagick fallback) to test GIMP saturation enhancement. Verification uses PIL's HSV color space analysis with four objective criteria: (1) minimum 15% relative saturation increase, (2) maximum 80% increase to avoid excess, (3) less than 5% pixel clipping at saturation max, and (4) minimum 10-point absolute increase on 0-255 scale. Task passes at 75% score (≥3 of 4 criteria). Recommended GIMP adjustment is +25 to +35 on the saturation slider. Testing confirms thresholds are well-calibrated: 1.2x enhancement satisfies all criteria with no clipping, while extreme 2.0x enhancement still passes with only 4.4% clipping. Task timeout is 90 seconds with 5-step limit.
